subject: Project Management
title: Black Swans in Projects
description: A fast breakdown of Black Swans in Projects for builders.

hook.text: Nassim Taleb coined 'Black Swan' for rare, unpredictable events with massive consequences.

buildup.text: In projects, Black Swans are the risks nobody planned for: pandemics, regulations, market collapses.

discovery.text: We can't predict Black Swans, but we can build projects that survive them — through buffers and flexibility.

twist.text: The real danger isn't the Black Swan itself — it's building brittle plans that assume none will ever happen.

climax.text: Taleb's lesson for projects: don't predict the unpredictable. Prepare for it.

punchline.text: You can't see the Black Swan coming. You can survive it.

quiz.question: What is a 'Black Swan' event?
options: A rare, unpredictable event with massive consequences
A common type of software bug
